channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Australian woman missing in US: Sisters share fears and updates
item.description: The sisters of Sally Contarino, an Australian woman who has been missing in the United States, have shared their concerns and updates regarding her disappearance. The article highlights their emotiona...

item.title: George Lucas discusses AI's impact on filmmaking through historical analogy
item.description: In an interview for Rabbit's Foot, Andy Hazel discusses George Lucas's views on generative AI in filmmaking. Lucas, creator of Star Wars and founder of the Lucas Museum of Narrative Art, draws a paral...

item.title: US Limits Foreign Students' Stay to Four Years Under New Visa Rules
item.description: The US Department of Homeland Security has introduced new visa rules limiting foreign students to a maximum of four years in the country. This change affects international students with F visas, cultu...

item.title: Fourth Suspect Surrenders in Marine Biologist's Killing, Police Hunt for Others
item.description: A fourth suspect in the killing of American marine biologist Kent Carpenter has surrendered to the police, according to a report by the Negros Oriental Provincial Police Office. The suspect, who serve...

item.title: Security Forces Conduct Joint Operations Against Terrorists in Bannu District
item.description: Security forces in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa's Bannu district and its surrounding areas have successfully neutralized 24 terrorists linked to the banned Tehreek-i-Taliban (TTP), according to a statement by t...

item.title: Nigeria aims to boost cashew processing to reduce raw exports
item.description: The Federal Government has pledged to transform Nigeria's cashew industry into a globally competitive sector by addressing the issue of 85% of raw cashew exports. At a stakeholders' workshop in Abuja,...

item.title: Ghana's Economic Shift: Airport City as West Africa's New Wealth Hub
item.description: The article explores how Ghana's Airport City is transforming into a major economic center in West Africa. Located near Accra International Airport, the area has attracted luxury developments, corpora...

item.title: China Condemns UK's Nationalisation of British Steel, Warns of Investment Treaty Breach
item.description: The Chinese commerce ministry has strongly opposed the UK government's decision to nationalise British Steel, accusing London of violating the China–UK Bilateral Investment Treaty. The move, which too...

item.title: Guinea's Plan to Boost Local Gold Refining Industry
item.description: Guinea has announced a ban on exporting raw gold, aiming to build a domestic refining industry to retain more value from its mineral resources. The decision, effective within 90 days, requires gold to...

item.title: Legal actions by Fadiel Adams lead to arrests of Khumalo and senior police officials
item.description: The article details how National Coloured Congress MP Fadiel Adams initiated multiple legal cases against former crime intelligence head Lt-Gen Dumisani Khumalo and other senior police officials, alle...

item.title: Philippines Leverages 2016 Ruling to Strengthen Maritime Security After Duterte's Inaction
item.description: The article discusses how the Philippines is utilizing the 2016 South China Sea Arbitral Award to bolster its maritime security strategies, despite former President Rodrigo Duterte's initial dismissal...

item.title: Sydney Airport launches paid priority security access for international flights
item.description: Sydney Airport has introduced a paid priority pass system allowing international travellers to bypass standard security and immigration queues. For $20, passengers can purchase a Fast Track pass in ad...

item.title: Kenyan Female Rally Driver Recognized for Excellence in Motorsport
item.description: Pauline Shegu, a prominent female rally driver in Kenya, has been nominated for the Outstanding Sports Personality award at the third edition of the International Women Parliament. This recognition hi...

item.title: US-Iran tensions drive oil prices higher amid Red Sea closure threats
item.description: Oil prices climbed on Friday as escalating tensions between the US and Iran disrupted Gulf operations, with the Strait of Hormuz facing reduced oil flows due to a broken truce. Brent crude futures ros...

item.title: Governor Mutfwang Reinstates Plateau Monarch, Boosts Traditional Leadership
item.description: Governor Caleb Manasseh Mutfwang has reinstated Alhaji Abdullahi Idris as the Rekna of Bashar, the highest traditional ruler in the district, after his suspension in 2024. The Bashar Chiefdom Communit...

item.title: 60 US Senators Propose 100% Tariffs on India for Purchasing Russian Oil
item.description: A new bill backed by 60 US Senators proposes imposing up to 100% tariffs on India and four other nations for purchasing Russian oil. The Sanctioning Russia Act of 2026, supported by President Donald T...

item.title: OAS Pension Rules Prevent Double Dipping for Retirees
item.description: The article explains that the Old Age Security (OAS) pension rules in Canada prevent retirees from benefiting from both residency bonuses and deferral bonuses simultaneously. A person who qualifies fo...

item.title: Namibian Citizens Repatriated from South Africa Seek Return Amid Xenophobic Tensions
item.description: The article discusses the repatriation of 72 Namibians from South Africa following xenophobic attacks, with many expressing hope to return once tensions subside. The Namibian government has stated it ...

item.title: Émile Vernon: Romantic French Artist of the Belle Époque
item.description: Émile Vernon (1872–1919) was a French artist renowned for his delicate and romantic style during the Belle Époque period. His works, primarily created in watercolor and gouache, captured the elegance ...

item.title: Wildlife Behavior Varies Based on Human Threat Type
item.description: A study conducted by researchers at the Indian Institute of Science (IISc) reveals that animals exhibit distinct behavioral responses to different types of human threats. The research highlights how s...
